overview: This deck leverages a low curve of aggressive Human creatures to apply immediate pressure while taxing the opponent's resource advantage. Signature pieces like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Thalia's Lieutenant slow down opposing strategies, allowing Adeline, Resplendent Cathar to grow into a massive threat. The game plan focuses on maintaining board presence and outlasting midrange or control decks through incremental value and combat tricks.
win_conditions: Overwhelming the opponent with early aggressive Humans
Growing Adeline, Resplendent Cathar into an unblockable threat
Taxing opponent spells until they run out of resources

keep_criteria: Hands with two- and three-drops that establish early board presence
Combinations featuring Thalia's Lieutenant or early Humans
Hands with removal or tax effects to disrupt opponent's early game
Keep hands with 1-2 lands and at least 3-4 one-mana Human creatures (e.g., Knight-Captain, Ambitious Assault, Thalia's Lieutenant, or early pump spells).
Prioritize hands that can play a creature on turn 1 and a second on turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early plays or curve breakers
Mana-heavy hands with only four-drops or higher
Hands missing synergistic Humans or tax effects
Mulligan if you have 3+ lands with no early threats, 0-1 lands with no recovery plan, or hands missing both lands and one-mana threats.

early_game: Play lands and cheap Humans to establish board presence
Prioritize Thalia's Lieutenant to tax opponent's early spells
Use combat tricks to maximize early damage
Turn 1: Play a Plains, cast a one-mana Human (Knight-Captain, Ambitious Assault, or Thalia's Lieutenant).
Turn 2: Play a Plains, cast a second Human or a pump spell (Rally the Righteous, Steelshaper's Gift).
If you have Thalia's Lieutenant, prioritize casting it to tax opponent non-Human spells.
Land sequencing: Play basic Plains first
if using fetch/shock, ensure you can hit basics for consistency.
mid_game: Deploy Thalia, Guardian of Thraben to further tax opponent's resources
Begin pumping Adeline, Resplendent Cathar with +1/+1 counters
Maintain board pressure while answering opponent's threats
Preserve mana for interaction and finishing blows.
Avoid overcommitting to the board if the opponent has sweepers or heavy removal.
Use combat to trigger lifelink/vigilance and maintain card advantage.
Protect your graveyard from discard effects and keep key pieces (especially Adeline) safe from targeted removal.
Commit threats when you have protection for Adeline or the opponent has no sweepers.
Hold back if the opponent has potential board wipes or heavy removal.
late_game: Focus on growing Adeline into a massive blocker and attacker
Use tax effects to stall opponent's finishers
Close out the game with combat damage or a pumped Adeline
Adeline, Resplendent Cathar (with pump/vigilance), and fully buffed Humans.
Win by trading efficiently, leveraging lifelink to stabilize, and using combat tricks to close out the game.
Adeline's +1/+1 and vigilance make her a formidable finisher that can outlast control and midrange decks.
Go all-in when you have protection for Adeline, the opponent has no sweepers, or you can trade up favorably.
Commit when you can close the game in 1-2 turns or when the opponent has exhausted their interaction.
Use combat tricks to maximize damage and secure lethal.

key_cards.name: Adeline, Resplendent Cathar
key_cards.notes: Prioritize pumping her with +1/+1 counters to make her unblockable and a major combat threat.

key_cards.name: Thalia's Lieutenant
key_cards.notes: Play early to increase opponent's spell costs, disrupting their curve and buying time for your Humans.

key_cards.name: Thalia, Guardian of Thraben
key_cards.notes: Deploy to further tax opponent's spells and provide a solid body that synergizes with your aggressive strategy.

sequencing_tips: Hold up mana for combat tricks to maximize early damage
Play tax creatures before opponent's key spells to maximize disruption
Pump Adeline only when you can follow up with combat or protection
Common mistakes include overcommitting to the board without protection for Adeline, ignoring opponent potential sweepers, and mismanaging mana for interaction.
Thalia's Lieutenant's tax applies to opponent spells, not yours, so use it to disrupt their game plan.
Adeline's +1/+1 and vigilance make her a formidable finisher, but she requires protection.
Use combat tricks to maximize damage and watch for format-specific removal and sweepers.
Practice tracking opponent interaction to know when to commit or hold back.

matchups.advice: Race early and use tax effects to slow their curve. Prioritize removal on their biggest threats.
Establish board presence before they stabilize. Use tax effects to disrupt their value engines.
Apply relentless pressure and use tax to delay their counters and sweepers. Grow Adeline quickly to force answers.

Use cheap, efficient threats that are hard to counter (1-mana Humans). Save interaction for key spells. Use pump spells or combat tricks to close out before counters can react. If possible, bring in more uncounterable threats or protection spells.

Apply early pressure to beat them out. Use removal to kill their mana dorks or key ramp creatures. Thalia's tax makes their ramp spells expensive, slowing them down. Force them to spend resources on interaction rather than acceleration.

Win by out-valuing. Use removal to kill their key pieces. Save counters for their Adeline or Thalia. Trade efficiently and use pump spells to win combat. Card advantage and protection are key. Look for opportunities to trade your less valuable creatures for their better ones.

common_threats: Board wipes that clear your Human army
Counterspells that stop your tax creatures
Watch for board wipes (Wrath of God, Day of Judgment), targeted removal (Path to Exile, Swords to Plowshares), and counterspells.
Note when the opponent taps out for a sweeper or heavy removal.
Track graveyard hate if facing combo/midrange.
Pay attention to when the opponent uses their last interaction, as this is your window to close out the game.
